title: luzula multiflora subsp. frigida
intro: Luzula multiflora subsp. frigida, also known as Frigid Woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae, characterized by its grass-like foliage and small white flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Luzula multiflora subsp. frigida include its ability to act as a natural antiseptic, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
